title: Multistage containerfile not running with Podman Quadlet
body:
### Issue Description

See https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/26841 for details.

The problem seems to be Podman Quadlet looping, Podman Desktop works just fine.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Build a multistage image using a Containerfile
2. Start using Podman Quadlet

### Describe the results you received

Cannot pull / looping / corruption

### Describe the results you expected

It does work fine with Podman Desktop, but I want to use this without GUI and in Podman Quadlet.

### Additional information

It works fine in Podman Desktop.

comments.body:
This issue seems to happen when using `UserNS=keep-id`.

It works fine without this. Is this expected?

Edit:

After some trial and error, it seems to be caused by the number of small files being created when running `composer install` and `pnpm install`. After moving those out of the image (to `entrypoint.sh` instead) and increasing the `timeout` value, they are run every time the container starts. This is a downside, but it solves the long waiting time and corruption caused when hitting timeout.

It didn't show up in my logging (even with debug log param), but this seems to be related: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/16541

I do understand why it crashes, but I didn't expect it would cause so many problems with the podman storage itself. Any workarounds to make this less pain?

Thanks!

Edit 2:

The first time it takes almost up to 5-8 mins., container restarts afterwards are a lot shorter. I have moved the package install process back to the Containerfile, and increased the `timeout` value of container instance.

You don't get any indication a chown process is running (or maybe it's disabled on my setup?), maybe this would solve the issue? Because it now looks like podman is hanging, but it's actually doing something in the background.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
